threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: When you still ask for a latte at the coffee shop instead of knowing it’s a flat white you’re after. Total giveaway.
Last night on the 133, a guy offered his takeaway to a jammed full bus. Turns out it was from the best Caribbean spot in Brixton. Talk about a journey.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I spotted a guy painting a mural on a crumbling wall while two kids played footy on the street – that’s Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a currywurst at 3 AM after an underground rave and then chill by the canal with a random group of artists.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they grimace at BART delays but still know exactly which taco truck to hit for a quick bite.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just saw a Muni bus turn the corner, but the driver had to pause for a street performer juggling flaming clubs—only in SF, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the street performers are just part of the plaza vibe, but locals know they’re often a cover for pickpockets. Keeps us on our toes.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still taking photos of the Sagrada Família from every angle, oblivious to the fact that the real beauty is found in the narrow streets of Gràcia.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of 'the Overground' like a true local. And that hesitation when ordering a coffee at the market? Tells everyone you're fresh off the boat.
Last night on the 36, a guy got on at Peckham Rye, dressed like he just stepped out of a 90s rave. Gave an impromptu DJ set the whole ride to Elephant.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Sitting in my kitchen, I heard a group of kids outside debating whether the BVG strike was worth skipping school. That's when it hit me.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find a rooftop pool party happening right next to a graffiti-covered former factory. The vibe? Unmatched and totally Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their eyes rolling at BART delays while sipping a coffee from La Mediterranee. Tourists just look confused.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo juggling on the 49 while someone next to him argued about the price of avocado toast. Classic San Francisco vibe.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think we all love the music festivals in Plaça del Nord, but ask any local about the noise at 3 AM on a Sunday.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You'll spot them struggling with the 'café con leche' order, still pronouncing it like 'ka-fay' instead of the local 'ka-feh'. It's the little slips that give them away.
